serviceDescription: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (AWS DRS) minimizes downtime and data loss with fast, reliable recovery of on-premises and cloud-based applications using affordable storage, minimal compute, and point-in-time recovery.

templates.title: Recovery replication
templates.description: Your monthly cost will be comprised of the charge for AWS DRS, as well as the additional AWS resources that AWS DRS uses to maintain ongoing replication and to enable drill or recovery launches, as described below.

cards.title: Replication
cards.bodyText: AWS DRS is billed hourly, per replicating server. The cost is the same, regardless of the number of disks, size of storage, number of drill or recovery launches, or the Region to which you are replicating.
cards.helpText: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (AWS DRS) has simple, predictable, usage-based pricing. With AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery, you pay only for the servers you are actively replicating to AWS. Your costs are based on a flat per hour fee. There are no resources to manage, no upfront costs, and no minimum fee. This gives you the flexibility to easily use our recovery solution by paying on an hourly basis, rather than committing to a long-term contract or set number of servers.

components.description: For all volumes (>=125GB) are priced as chosen EBS volume type from the dropdown list. All the rest (<125GB) are priced as standard disks (EBS Magnetic).

options.label: Estimated cost - General purpose SSD (gp3) & Cold HDD (sc1) & Magnetic (previous generation)
Minimum cost - Cold HDD (sc1) & Magnetic (previous generation)
Maximum cost - General purpose SSD (gp3) & Magnetic (previous generation)

components.text: Calculation for minimum cost: All volumes equal or larger than 125GB are priced as sc1, all the rest (<125GB), priced as standard disks
Calculation for maximum cost: All volumes equal or larger than 125GB are priced as gp3, all the rest (<125GB), priced as standard disks

Calculation for estimated cost: The volume is dynamically modified from gp3 to sc1 (and back) based on the average write rate using 12MiB/s per 1TiB as the threshold (See EBS documentation for more details). Estimated cost calculation will be determined by the average write rate range your usage falls into.

* Thresholds are dynamic based on the calculated average disk size. The example above refers to 1TiB average disk size. For X TiB, the Min and Max should be multiplied by X.

components.description: The default retention policy is 7 days.

components.label: info alert
components.content:
AWS DRS uses EC2 instances as Replication Servers, which facilitate ongoing data replication. By default, these are low-cost t3.small EC2 instances. Each Replication Server can handle replication of disks from multiple source servers, with a maximum of 15 staging disks. For some high write rate source servers, you may choose to use more performant instance types as Replication Servers. Replication Servers are launched and terminated by AWS DRS automatically, as needed.

For EC2 On-Demand instance hourly rate, please refer to EC2 Pricing page for the most-up-to-date price.

To estimate EC2 charge, please choose 'Add service' to add EC2 calculator on My Estimate page after saving DRS estimate to there. My Estimate page will display total monthly and annual cost of DRS, EBS and EC2.

By default, the volumes per replication server is 15 and applied to below calculation for 'Number of replication servers' (='Number of disks' divided by 15). Apply the result directly to the input field 'Quantity' in EC2 calculator - Quick estimate for EC2 cost.
